title: Introducing Chunking for Large Files
summary: Chunking reads and processes large files in smaller pieces so memory usage stays stable.

sections.heading: What is Chunking?
sections.content: Chunking is a technique where a large file is read and processed in smaller, manageable pieces instead of loading the entire file into memory at once. Each chunk is processed sequentially and can be released before the next chunk is loaded.

sections.heading: Why is Chunking Necessary?
points: Datasets can exceed the RAM capacity of the environment.
Incremental processing keeps memory use stable and reduces crash risk.

sections.heading: How it Works
sections.content: You choose a chunksize, iterate over each chunk, process it, and then combine aggregated results after all chunks are handled.

sections.heading: Benefits
points: Ability to process very large datasets
